d) this is an example of range partitioning.
e) this is an example of list partitioning.

questions.question: In the design of OODB the objects can be grouped in:

answers.text: a) Composite objects = they have at least one multi-value property and all the properties are not objects
b) Compound objects = they have at least one multi-value property and all the properties are not objects
c) Aggregated objects = objects that represent groups of entities and contain group properties that are transmitted to all instances of subclasses (inheritance).
d) Simple objects = they have single-valued properties (domain with one value) and have properties which are objects
e) Associated objects = independent objects that are designed to establish a link between other objects

b) The "id" is the partitioning key.
c) this is an example of list partitioning.
d) The "garden" is the partitioning key.
e) this is an example of hash partitioning.

questions.question: Choose the correct statement(s), referring to indexes.
